quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
stewart_read: The call never connected to a human. The transcript is entirely an automated voicemail greeting with no rep response captured. There is no strategic-vs-reactive distinction to make because there is no rep behavior to evaluate.
coaching_implication: Nothing to coach from this transcript itself. The manager should verify in the dialer system whether the rep left a voicemail message after the beep — that is the only open question. If no message was left, the coaching conversation is about voicemail discipline: does the rep have a standard leave-a-message protocol, and are they using it consistently on no-answer attempts?

quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
stewart_read: Lead type is unknown for this call. On a paid lead, a no-contact attempt still consumes a dial against a lead that requires a bill before the appointment can be set. If this is a paid lead and the rep is not leaving a structured callback message, the sit opportunity is leaking without any recovery mechanism.
coaching_implication: Manager should confirm lead type in the CRM. If paid lead: verify whether a voicemail was left and whether a follow-up attempt is scheduled. The codex flags that Spencer's son missed 5 sits in one day on paid leads where bill collection stalled — a no-contact with no follow-up protocol compounds that risk.

quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
stewart_read: No bill collection is possible on a voicemail intercept. If the rep's only follow-up path is a personal phone number or a manual callback, this lead is at risk of going cold with no systematic recovery. The codex's automated bill-capture roadmap item is directly relevant here — a structured SMS follow-up triggered from the dialer would give this lead a recovery path the rep cannot provide manually.
coaching_implication: Use this call as a concrete example when discussing the automated bill-capture capability with Kenny. The no-contact scenario is exactly where systematic follow-up outperforms rep-by-rep manual effort.

quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
stewart_read: The transcript capture itself is the signal here. A recording that ends at the voicemail greeting — with no rep audio — may indicate a transcription cutoff, a rep who hung up immediately, or a dialer that dropped the call at the voicemail detection. Each has a different operational implication and none can be resolved from this transcript alone.
coaching_implication: Flag for ops review: is the transcription system capturing post-beep rep audio on voicemail calls? If the rep left a message and it was not captured, the floor is losing coaching signal on every no-answer attempt. If the rep hung up without leaving a message, that is a separate training gap. The manager cannot determine which without checking the dialer recording directly.
